phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Fed and RBA (Reserve Bank of Australia) policy data. Fetch and report: - Fed current stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - RBA current stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from OIS swap pricing or ASX 30-Day Interbank Cash Rate futures — exact %) - Fed Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the Fed Funds rate expected to settle by year-end? (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or consensus forecasts — exact %) - RBA Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the RBA Cash Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from ASX futures pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - Impact of the latest quarterly or monthly Australian CPI print on RBA rate expectations — did it shift the market's rate path pricing? State clearly: did CPI come in hotter or softer than expected, and how has that moved RBA cut/hike probabilities? - Any Fed or RBA speaker comments in the last 48 hours (include RBA Governor, Deputy Governor, or Board member statements) - Which central bank is more hawkish right now and why ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - RBA Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Rate Differential by Year-End: [Fed Terminal – RBA Terminal = bps gap] — widening or narrowing vs current? - CPI Impact on RBA Path: [hotter/softer/in-line] | RBA Cut Probability Shift: [increased/decreased/unchanged] | Detail: [one line] - Key RBA Speaker Note: [most recent RBA comment in 48h or "None"] - Key Fed Speaker Note: [most recent Fed comment in 48h or "None"] - Dominant CB: [Fed/RBA/Equal] - AUDUSD Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - POLICY DIVERGENCE] - Fed Outlook: [stance + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - RBA Outlook: [stance + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - Year-End Rate Gap: [Fed terminal – RBA terminal in bps, widening/narrowing] - CPI Impact on RBA: [hotter/softer/in-line → cut probability shift direction] - Key Speaker Note: [most recent Fed/RBA comment in 48h, or None] - Core Logic: [why this divergence + terminal rate gap matters for AUDUSD today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): SECTION A — US DATA: - US: CPI, NFP, GDP, PMI — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) SECTION B — AUSTRALIAN DOMESTIC DATA: - Employment Change (monthly) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Unemployment Rate — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Wage Price Index (quarterly) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Retail Sales (monthly) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Monthly CPI Indicator (monthly) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Australian PMI (Manufacturing and/or Services) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) SECTION C — CHINESE ECONOMIC DATA (China is a critical macro proxy for the Australian economy — AUD is highly sensitive to Chinese demand): - China Manufacturing PMI (NBS and/or Caixin) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - China Services PMI — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - China GDP (quarterly) — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - China Retail Sales — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - China Industrial Production — Actual vs Forecast vs Previous (mark "Data Unavailable" if not released) - Any notable Chinese stimulus announcements, PBOC actions, or government spending signals in the last 5 days - Citigroup Economic Surprise Index for USD and AUD (exact values — positive = beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact AU or China data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for AUDUSD 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - AU Domestic Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - China Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) | China-AUD Proxy Signal: [Supportive/Bearish/Neutral for AUD] - Citi USD Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi AUD Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Fundamental Upper Hand: [USD / AUD / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC MOMENTUM] - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - AU Domestic Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - China Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data + beat/miss] | China-AUD Proxy: [Supportive/Bearish/Neutral] - Citi USD Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi AUD Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [which economy is outperforming, how China data amplifies the AUD outlook, and what it means for AUDUSD] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest AUDUSD positioning, sentiment, and risk appetite data. Fetch and report: - CFTC COT report: NET Non-Commercial (speculative) AUD futures contracts — exact number, long or short. Is it at a historical extreme? Use this data strictly for identifying the overarching macro trend context and whether the smart money trend is gaining or fading. Do NOT use COT for short-term intraday signals. - IG Client Sentiment: exact % of retail traders Long vs Short AUDUSD right now. Weight this data higher for short-term and intraday contrarian setups — when retail is heavily skewed one way (e.g., 70%+ short), apply a contrarian lean for today's intraday bias. State clearly: is a contrarian intraday opportunity present? - CRITICAL — S&P 500 direction and VIX level: AUD is a well-established "risk-on" currency. When the S&P 500 is rising and VIX is falling (risk-on environment), this is fundamentally supportive for AUDUSD. When the S&P 500 is falling and VIX is spiking (risk-off environment), this is a direct headwind for AUDUSD. State clearly: what is the current risk environment and how does it directionally impact AUDUSD? - CNN Fear & Greed Index — current level and reading - Is retail sentiment at a contrarian extreme? State clearly Yes or No, and if Yes, state the contrarian directional implication for intraday AUDUSD ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. 
📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — AUDUSD (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for AUDUSD. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant AUDUSD options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - AUDUSD NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - AUDUSD Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish AUDUSD — magnet above / Bearish AUDUSD — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (AUDUSD): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net AUD: [contracts + long/short] | Macro Trend Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral macro positioning] - IG Retail: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - S&P 500: [direction + % move] | VIX: [level + direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off/Neutral] | AUDUSD Impact: [Tailwind/Headwind/Neutral] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Contrarian] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT & POSITIONING] - CFTC Non-Comm Net AUD: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Macro Context: [trend direction] - IG Retail Sentiment: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - S&P 500: [direction %] | VIX: [level] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off/Neutral] | AUDUSD Impact: [Tailwind/Headwind/Neutral] - Core Logic: [contrarian intraday signal from IG + macro trend context from COT + risk-on/risk-off S&P 500 signal — which edge applies today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Contrarian] ```
phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest macro and geopolitical risks affecting AUDUSD, with specific focus on Australian commodity drivers and Chinese macroeconomic conditions. Fetch and report: - Iron Ore price (exact level in USD/tonne) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%) — Iron Ore is Australia's largest export commodity and one of the single most powerful drivers of the Australian Dollar. Rising Iron Ore = bullish AUD tailwind; Falling Iron Ore = bearish AUD headwind. State the directional implication clearly. - Copper price (exact level in USD/lb or USD/tonne) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%) — Copper is a key barometer of global industrial demand and Chinese economic health, both of which directly drive AUD. State the directional implication for AUDUSD. - Gold (XAUUSD) current price, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and its sentiment/trend context — is Gold rising (safe-haven demand / risk-off) or falling (risk appetite returning / risk-on)? phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear AUDUSD institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Policy Divergence (Fed vs RBA): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Yield Differentials (US vs AU Bonds): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Momentum (AU Domestic + China Proxy): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Risk Appetite: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Commodities & China Macro Risk: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity. Note that AUDUSD is also highly active during the Sydney/Tokyo Asian session where commodity price moves and Chinese data can reprice the pair sharply.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
